_provenance.stage: Stage 6 — Synthesis (retroactive rebuild from Codex Stage 3)
version_history: v2 (2026-05-17): retroactive editorial pass. Stage 3 (Codex) FAS=72 with 12 verified claims — strongest verified core of any 3R-adjacent issue in the audit. Multiple OVERCLAIM cardinal-sin flags addressed by switching from assertive to attributive framing throughout: 'declared deposed' → 'purported to depose', 'three hold office; one was dismissed' → state government's account attributed to MB. Article-citation specifics dropped (BERNAMA cites the relevant articles is sufficient; the precise Article 10-12 / 10-13 cross-citation was unverified). 'JAIS and police were notified' replaced with verified 'police reports were made' and named-source Telaga Undang Sarifah Norzaidah called the act menderhaka. Reframe and view softened from interpretive (defended the throne first) to factual (some institutions rejected the declaration). 3R-ROYAL — higher verification bar applied throughout.

revision_log: CORRECTED (Stage 3): OVERCLAIM — Headline 'Four Undangs Declared the Ruler Deposed. Three Hold Office. One Does Not.' → 'Four Undangs Purported to Depose the Ruler. State Government Says One Was No Longer in Office.' (proper attribution to state-government account).

CORRECTED (Stage 3): OVERCLAIM — Hook 'declared Tuanku Muhriz deposed and named Tunku Nadzaruddin the 12th Ruler' → 'signed a declaration purporting to depose Tuanku Muhriz' (proper attribution; the state government and constitutional analysts dispute that the action took effect).
REPHRASED (Stage 3): OVERCLAIM — 'Three hold office; one was dismissed' → 'The MB said that Undang had been dismissed effective 13 May 2025' (attribution to state government's account).

REMOVED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — Specific article-citation cross-reference 'BERNAMA and Malay Mail cite Articles 10-12; The Star cites 10-13' dropped; only verified 'BERNAMA cites the relevant articles' retained.

REPHRASED (Stage 3): OVERCLAIM — 'All agree: rejection rests on Mubarak no longer holding office' broadened to acknowledge both the status-of-Mubarak ground and later legal-analysis question about full-and-complete enquiry.
REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'Mubarak was dismissed 13 May 2025 for 33 offences against Telaga Undang custom' attributed to MB account and expanded to include the other named customs.
REPHRASED (Stage 3): OVERCLAIM — 'The DKU ratified the dismissal' → 'BERNAMA: the DKU sitting accepted the Telaga Undang consensus dismissing Mubarak' (sourcing the specific action).

CORRECTED (Stage 3): INCORRECT/UNVERIFIED — 'Institutions called the act menderhaka; JAIS and police were notified' → 'Telaga Undang Sarifah Norzaidah called Mubarak's action treasonous (menderhaka). Police reports were made.' (named source; JAIS notification was unverified).

REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED absence-claim — 'No state, customary, or judicial body has publicly addressed the misconduct claim itself' softened to 'No public adjudication of the underlying misconduct claim was located'.
REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Reframe expanded from one-question framing to two-questions framing (was enquiry complete + can three Undangs invoke alone).

CORRECTED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED interpretive — View 'institutions have defended the throne first' → 'Some institutions rejected the declaration and defended Tuanku Muhriz's position. No court has ruled. The constitutional and customary questions remain open.' (sourced and balanced).

PRESERVED (Stage 3 VERIFIED): 19 April 2026 four-Undang declaration; Tunku Nadzaruddin proposed as 12th Ruler; same Undang's 2008 proclamation; 1967 throne-bypass history; Tunku Ja'afar / Nadzaruddin lineage; collective Undang power to call Ruler to withdraw; constitutional question of three-alone untested.
